title: Split progression service into stable submodules
goal: Remove the remaining server-side serial bottleneck by extracting progression preview, commit, runtime projection, and helper queries into dedicated files.
verification_steps: `pnpm check` passes. | `pnpm test` passes. | No behavior regressions in spend-plan parse, preview, commit, or runtime projection tests. | Each exported progression operation lives in a focused module with a small public surface.
prompt_hints: Own only `app/src/server/progression/**` and the listed test file. | Prefer extraction and naming cleanup over behavioral changes. | Leave mechanic status untouched unless the batch materially changes functionality.

title: Expand SRD roster-critical canon
goal: Finish the SRD canon needed by the seeded roster and by the immediate runtime batches: spells, class features, feats, species, and equipment actually in play.
mechanic_ids: spell-lookup-by-id | spell-lookup-by-name-alias | spell-roster-canon-coverage | spell-slot-pools-from-class-features | spell-pact-magic-progression | feature-primal-order-warden | feature-second-wind-surface | feature-bardic-inspiration-surface | feature-font-of-magic-points | species-drow-lineage-spells | species-wood-elf-druidcraft
verification_steps: `pnpm compile` passes. | Converted entity counts match the batch manifest. | Runtime lookup resolves the roster-critical entities without fallback notes. | At least one straightforward, one edge-case, and one cross-reference entity are spot-checked.
prompt_hints: Treat canonical files as source of truth; do not edit generated outputs by hand. | Flag every judgment call in canonical metadata when modernization is required. | Do not widen scope to monsters or non-roster content in this batch.

title: Expand AA overlays and AA ability canon
goal: Increase AA coverage around the roster and immediate hybrid-progression path while preserving explicit judgment metadata and pack provenance.
verification_steps: `pnpm compile` passes. | Every AA modernization that is not a direct carry-over is marked as a judgment call. | Overlay references and derived-from references resolve cleanly. | Enabled-pack lookup exposes AA entities only when the pack is enabled.
prompt_hints: Own only the AA pack directory plus compile-adjacent validation touched by AA coverage. | Do not edit SRD base files unless a validator change truly requires it. | Keep AA content additive; do not fork the runtime around AA-specific branches.

title: Build the acceptance and status harness
goal: Turn status changes into evidence by adding live-roster snapshot checks, fixture coverage, and report generation that agents can update safely.
owned_paths: data/mechanics-coverage/ | data/fleet/ | docs/reports/ | scripts/report-mechanics-coverage.ts | scripts/report-fleet-readiness.ts | worklog.md
verification_steps: Tracker and fleet reports generate deterministically. | A live-roster snapshot command exists and is documented. | Status flips from `partial` to `full` require linked evidence in tests or snapshots. | The worklog records the new baseline and next-wave assignments.
prompt_hints: This batch owns the reporting artifacts and status bookkeeping. | Do not mark mechanics `full` just because content exists; require runtime and evidence. | Prefer machine-generated docs over hand-edited status tables.

title: Add choice-state and equipment persistence
goal: Persist skill picks, feat picks, spell picks, owned equipment, and equipped state so runtime can stop relying on sheet baselines.
mechanic_ids: core-skill-choice-capture | feat-magic-initiate-choice-capture | feat-skilled-choice-capture | action-equipment-persistence | action-equipped-state-persistence | action-weapon-mastery-choice-capture | feature-metamagic-option-capture | feature-pact-blade-bond-state
verification_steps: New schema and migrations apply cleanly. | Seeded characters can be reseeded idempotently with structured choices and equipment state. | Runtime projection no longer needs note-only fallbacks for the covered choices. | At least one roster character proves each new choice-state bucket.
prompt_hints: Keep the write scope inside progression schema/service and seed data. | Prefer additive tables or columns that preserve auditability over opaque JSON blobs. | Do not mix UI work into this batch.

title: Close the skill, expertise, and passive loop
goal: Use persisted choices to derive full skill state, expertise, and passives from sources rather than photographed baselines.
verification_steps: Computed skill values and passives match the reviewed roster for covered characters. | Explanations cite the exact source rows or canonical entities that produced the result. | No covered character still needs a skill or passive baseline override.
prompt_hints: Own only the proficiencies module, related runtime types, tests, and roster fixtures. | Keep the output explainable; do not hide fallback numbers in opaque payloads. | Update statuses only for the mechanics explicitly covered here.

title: Add resource spend, restore, and rest reset flows
goal: Make resources stateful and recoverable so session use stops depending on paper tracking.
mechanic_ids: action-fixed-resource-pools | action-scaling-resource-pools | action-resource-spend-mutation | action-resource-restore-mutation | action-short-rest-reset-engine | action-long-rest-reset-engine | rules-rest-flow-event-recording
verification_gates: persistence | runtime | mutation | tests | fixtures | live-roster
verification_steps: Resources have current/max state persisted outside sheet notes. | Short and long rest flows reset the right pools and record an event. | At least one live character proves short-rest recovery and one proves long-rest recovery.
prompt_hints: Keep this batch centered on resource state and rest semantics, not attack or spell resolution. | If a feature needs a resource but not full execution, land the state model first. | Preserve auditability of every spend and reset action.

title: Derive AC, equipment effects, and attack profiles from equipped state
goal: Replace AC fallbacks and add real attack surfaces for weapons and similar actions.

verification_steps: AC is fully explained from equipped items and formulas for covered characters. | Weapon attacks show derived attack bonus, damage, and relevant tags. | Weapon Mastery appears only when the character has both the feature and a valid weapon context.
prompt_hints: Keep this batch focused on equipment-to-runtime projection. | Do not implement full combat sequencing here; build derived attack surfaces first. | Use roster equipment before broad compendium breadth.

title: Add condition state, mechanical effects, and DM overrides
